Specifies files and directories to ignore in version control for the spam-detection project.

# Purpose
This file is a `.gitignore` configuration file used in a software project to specify which files and directories Git should ignore. It includes patterns for various types of files that are typically generated during the build process and are not necessary to track in version control. These patterns include object files (`*.o`, `*.obj`), linker outputs (`*.map`, `*.exp`), precompiled headers (`*.gch`, `*.pch`), libraries (`*.lib`, `*.a`), shared objects (`*.dll`, `*.so`), executables (`*.exe`, `*.out`), and debug files (`*.dSYM/`, `*.pdb`). Additionally, it includes patterns for kernel module compile results, such as `*.mod*` and `modules.order`, which are also not needed in the repository. By ignoring these files, the `.gitignore` file helps keep the repository clean and focused on source files.
